text: Being here, I feel a sense of quiet anticipation. The first space is open yet oddly impersonal, as if waiting for purpose to fill it. The beanbags hint at comfort, but the starkness and emptiness allow my mind to drift. In the second space, words and screens surround me; there is activity, creativity, and inquiry. I am wrestled gently between engagement and observation, reminded of the dynamic interplay between human thoughts and evolving environments. Curiosity stirs, but so does a quiet detachment, an urge to contemplate rather than immerse.
